title: Question: install and runtime problems
body:
---


Author Name: **Mark Jackson Pulver**
Original Redmine Message: 1201 from https://www.veripool.org



---

This is my first time using verilator.  I might have  missed a simple step.
I have two problems which I might not be related. 

Running under Ubunto 12.04 : Linux t04-shell 3.2.0-49-generic-pae #75-Ubuntu SMP Tue Jun 18 18:00:21 UTC 2013 i686 i686 i386 GNU/Linux


1) During 'make test' of the install it fails test_sc, but passes the verilog testing before this point.
```
[53] t_clk: Passed
[53] t_inst: MODE = 3  IS=1 OS=0
[53] 1 1 1 3 3f_ffffffff_ffffffff
All Tests passed
make[1]: Leaving directory `/home/mark/projects/etools/verilator/test_c'
make[1]: Entering directory `/home/mark/projects/etools/verilator/test_sc'
/usr/bin/perl /home/mark/projects/etools/verilator/test_sc/../bin/verilator             --sc -f /home/mark/projects/etools/verilator/test_sc/../test_v/input.vc top.v --trace
%Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry.  Consider trying --debug --gdbbt
%Error: Command Failed /home/mark/projects/etools/verilator/test_sc/../verilator_bin --sc -f /home/mark/projects/etools/verilator/test_sc/../test_v/input.vc top.v --trace
make[1]: *** [prep] Error 255
make[1]: Leaving directory `/home/mark/projects/etools/verilator/test_sc'
make: *** [test_sc] Error 2

```

Trying to debug the next problem I tried building an old version 3_840, it also failed 'make test' also on test_sc but much later at a different spot.
```
g++     -L/home/mark/projects/etools/systemc-2.3.0/lib-linux  -g sc_main.o verilated.o verilated_vcd_c.o verilated_vcd_sc.o Vtop__ALL.a /home/mark/projects/etools/systemc-2.3.0/lib-linux/libsystemc.a    -o simx -lsystemc -lm -lstdc++ 2>&1 | c++filt
/home/mark/projects/etools/systemc-2.3.0/lib-linux/libsystemc.a(sc_prim_channel.o): In function `sc_core::sc_host_mutex::trylock()':
sc_prim_channel.cpp:(.text._ZN7sc_core13sc_host_mutex7trylockEv[sc_core::sc_host_mutex::trylock()]+0xe): undefined reference to `pthread_mutex_trylock'
collect2: ld returned 1 exit status
make[2]: Leaving directory `/home/mark/projects/etools/verilator_3_840/test_sc/obj_dir'
obj_dir/simx
make[1]: obj_dir/simx: Command not found
make[1]: *** [run] Error 127
make[1]: Leaving directory `/home/mark/projects/etools/verilator_3_840/test_sc'
make: *** [test_sc] Error 2

```

I have not tried to debug this yet, as I don't need SC until my DUT is ready...

2) Assuming my install is good enough to try verilator on my verilog DUT, I'll tried to lint the code

I found verilator would dump core on my entire design:
```
No stack.
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
%Error: Command Failed /home/mark/projects/etools/verilator/verilator_bin '-DVERILATOR=1' -y ./bbrx -y ./bsearch -y ./dmay -y ./mce -y ./misc -y ./rif -y ./sdr -y ./Xilinx_prims -y ./mcb_raw_wrapper -y /home/mark/projects/modules/mce -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod -y /home/mark/projects/modules/hatch_modules/dcom_lpcb -y /home/mark/projects/modules/hatch_modules/dcom_uart -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod/octal_sample_fifo -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod/octal_ce -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod/octal_control -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod/octal_message -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod/octal_calculate_angle -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod/octal_peak_mag -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod/octal_pll -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod/octal_freq_correct -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod/octal_sync_fifo -y /home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod/octal_time_sort_fifo -y /home/mark/projects/modules/peak_detect_if -y /home/mark/projects/modules/am32 -y /home/mark/projects/modules/debug_capture_16k -y /home/mark/projects/modules/lpf_4M8Hz_90Mss -y /home/mark/projects/modules/cfc -y /home/mark/projects/modules/gps_parse -y /home/mark/projects/modules/gps_rx_uart -y /home/mark/projects/modules/spi -y /home/mark/projects/modules/fft_n64 -y /home/mark/projects/modules/fft_interference_cancellation -y /home/mark/projects/modules/ifft_n64 -y /home/mark/projects/modules/block_ram -y /home/mark/projects/modules/ram_1024x36 -y /home/mark/projects/modules/ram_4096x36 -y /home/mark/projects/modules/ram_1024x18 -y /home/mark/projects/modules/ram_512x36 -y /home/mark/projects/modules/ram_1024x32 -y /home/mark/projects/modules/ram_512x36_two_dual_ports -y . -y /opt/Xilinx/13.3/ISE_DS/ISE/verilog/src -y ./Xilinx_prims -I/home/mark/projects/modules/mce -I/home/mark/projects/modules/include -I/home/mark/projects/modules/hatch_modules/dcom_lpcb -I/home/mark/projects/modules/octal_demod -I. -Wall --lint-only --bbox-unsup --gdbbt daffy.v

```

It would lint my leaf cells in isolation, but even wraping a leaf cell, the wrapped version would cause verilator to dump core.

Tried building an older version 3_840, and found it was much less liking to dump core.  It fails only the top module
and one submodule (structural), but all other leaves and structure would pass.

Any ideas where I look next.


Mark
